description_long: This object is a small, vintage-style airplane with a single propeller mounted at the nose and a streamlined fuselage. The wings are straight and positioned low on the body, with simple, angular wingtips and no visible winglets. The engine is exposed on top of the nose, featuring multiple visible cylinders, suggesting a classic piston engine design. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a standard tail configuration. The landing gear consists of two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel, indicating a taildragger setup. The cockpit is located just behind the engine, appearing to be a single-seat, open or minimally enclosed design.
description: A small, vintage-style single-propeller airplane with exposed engine cylinders, low straight wings, and a taildragger landing gear. The cockpit is positioned just behind the engine and appears to be single-seat.
description_view_0: Front view showing the single propeller, exposed engine cylinders, and the symmetrical low wings.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the exposed engine, cockpit placement, and the main landing gear under the wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the low-wing configuration.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the taildragger landing gear, tail structure, and the streamlined fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single exposed piston engine mounted at the nose.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine, appears open or minimally enclosed.
